title: Auth test format_auth_header fails due to map key ordering
description:
The tests in apps/parrot_sip/test/parrot_sip/auth_test.exs for format_auth_header/1 fail intermittently due to map key ordering:

```
Assertion with == failed
code:  assert header == ~s(Digest realm="atlanta.com", nonce="abc123")
left:  "Digest nonce=\"abc123\", realm=\"atlanta.com\""
right: "Digest realm=\"atlanta.com\", nonce=\"abc123\""
```

The test compares exact string equality but Elixir maps don't guarantee key order. The test should either:
1. Parse the formatted header and compare components
2. Use a sorted output in format_auth_header
3. Use a map-based comparison instead of string comparison

Affected tests:
- test format_auth_header/1 formats basic auth parameters
- test format_auth_header/1 excludes nil values
close_reason: Fixed in commit c4df2bd - sorted auth parameters for consistent ordering

title: Dialog State Replication Infrastructure
description:
Implement Phoenix.PubSub-based dialog state replication for cluster support (Kamailio DMQ / Phoenix.Presence style).

## Overview
Enable ParrotSip to run in a cluster where any node can handle requests for any dialog. Uses in-memory replication with async broadcast - no external dependencies.

## Architecture
```
     Node A                              Node B
┌─────────────────┐                ┌─────────────────┐
│ DialogStatem    │                │ DialogStatem    │
│ (local process) │                │ (local process) │
└────────┬────────┘                └────────┬────────┘
         │                                  │
         ▼                                  ▼
┌─────────────────┐                ┌─────────────────┐
│ DialogStore.ETS │◄── PubSub ───►│ DialogStore.ETS │
│ (full replica)  │   broadcast    │ (full replica)  │
└─────────────────┘                └─────────────────┘
```

## Key Design Decisions
1. Processes remain local (timer precision)
2. State replicated to all nodes (availability)
3. Async writes (performance)
4. Sync on dialog creation (consistency for 200 OK)
5. Behaviour pattern for pluggable backends

## Failure Handling
- Node monitor detects :nodedown
- Surviving node has full replica
- ClusterMonitor triggers takeover
- New DialogStatem spawned with recovered state

## References
- Kamailio DMQ module
- Phoenix.Presence CRDT pattern
- RFC 3261 dialog state machine

title: Add integration tests for cluster failover
description:
Create integration tests that verify dialog recovery works across nodes.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. PubSub Replication Test
```elixir
test "dialog state is replicated via PubSub" do
  # Subscribe to replication topic
  Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(ParrotSip.PubSub, "dialog_replication")
  
  # Store a dialog
  dialog = build_test_dialog("replicated-1")
  DialogStore.store(dialog.id, dialog)
  
  # Should receive broadcast
  assert_receive {:dialog_stored, "replicated-1", ^dialog, _node}
end
```

### 2. Recovery Simulation Test
```elixir
test "ClusterMonitor recovers dialogs from dead node" do
  # Store a dialog owned by fake "dead" node
  dialog = build_test_dialog("orphan-1", owning_node: :fake_dead_node)
  DialogStore.store(dialog.id, dialog)
  
  # Simulate nodedown event
  send(ClusterMonitor, {:nodedown, :fake_dead_node, []})
  
  # Wait for recovery
  Process.sleep(100)
  
  # Dialog should now have a running process
  assert {:ok, pid} = DialogStatem.whereis(dialog.id)
  assert Process.alive?(pid)
  
  # Ownership should be transferred
  {:ok, updated} = DialogStore.fetch(dialog.id)
  assert updated.owning_node == node()
end
```

### 3. Multi-Node Test (requires distributed test setup)
```elixir
@tag :distributed
test "dialog survives node failure" do
  # This test requires LocalCluster or similar
  # Start two nodes
  [node1, node2] = LocalCluster.start_nodes("parrot", 2)
  
  # Create dialog on node1
  dialog_id = :rpc.call(node1, TestHelper, :create_dialog, [])
  
  # Verify node2 has replica
  assert {:ok, _} = :rpc.call(node2, DialogStore, :fetch, [dialog_id])
  
  # Kill node1
  LocalCluster.stop_nodes([node1])
  
  # Wait for recovery
  Process.sleep(500)
  
  # Dialog should be recovered on node2
  assert {:ok, pid} = :rpc.call(node2, DialogStatem, :whereis, [dialog_id])
  assert :rpc.call(node2, Process, :alive?, [pid])
end
```

title: Implement DialogStore.ETS (local-only)
description:
Create the ETS-based DialogStore implementation for single-node deployments.

## Purpose
Simple, fast implementation for:
- Development and testing
- Single-node production deployments
- Base layer for Replicated implementation

## Implementation

```elixir
defmodule ParrotSip.DialogStore.ETS do
  @behaviour ParrotSip.DialogStore
  
  use GenServer
  
  @table __MODULE__
  
  def start_link(opts) do
    GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, opts, name: __MODULE__)
  end
  
  def init(_opts) do
    table = :ets.new(@table, [:named_table, :public, :set, read_concurrency: true])
    {:ok, %{table: table}}
  end
  
  # Sync store
  @impl true
  def store(dialog_id, state) do
    record = {dialog_id, state, node(), DateTime.utc_now()}
    :ets.insert(@table, record)
    :ok
  end
  
  # Async store (same as sync for ETS, but matches behaviour)
  @impl true
  def store_async(dialog_id, state) do
    store(dialog_id, state)
  end
  
  @impl true
  def fetch(dialog_id) do
    case :ets.lookup(@table, dialog_id) do
      [{^dialog_id, state, _node, _time}] -> {:ok, state}
      [] -> {:error, :not_found}
    end
  end
  
  @impl true
  def delete(dialog_id) do
    :ets.delete(@table, dialog_id)
    :ok
  end
  
  @impl true
  def list_by_node(target_node) do
    :ets.select(@table, [{{:_, :\, target_node, :_}, [], [:\]}])
  end
  
  @impl true
  def list_all do
    :ets.select(@table, [{{:_, :\, :_, :_}, [], [:\]}])
  end
  
  @impl true
  def transfer_ownership(dialog_id, new_node) do
    case fetch(dialog_id) do
      {:ok, state} ->
        updated = %{state | owning_node: new_node, updated_at: DateTime.utc_now()}
        store(dialog_id, updated)
      error -> error
    end
  end
end
```

title: Implement DialogStore.Replicated (PubSub broadcast)
description:
Create the PubSub-based DialogStore implementation for multi-node clusters.

## Purpose
Enable dialog state replication across cluster nodes using Phoenix.PubSub broadcast (similar to Kamailio DMQ).

## Architecture
```
Local writes ──► ETS (local) ──► PubSub broadcast ──► Other nodes update their ETS
```

## Implementation

```elixir
defmodule ParrotSip.DialogStore.Replicated do
  @behaviour ParrotSip.DialogStore
  
  use GenServer
  
  @table :parrot_dialog_store
  @pubsub ParrotSip.PubSub
  @topic "dialog_replication"
  
  def start_link(opts) do
    GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, opts, name: __MODULE__)
  end
  
  def init(_opts) do
    # Create ETS table for local replica
    table = :ets.new(@table, [:named_table, :public, :set, read_concurrency: true])
    
    # Subscribe to replication topic
    Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(@pubsub, @topic)
    
    {:ok, %{table: table}}
  end
  
  # Sync store - write locally then broadcast
  @impl true
  def store(dialog_id, state) do
    record = {dialog_id, state, node(), DateTime.utc_now()}
    :ets.insert(@table, record)
    
    # Broadcast to other nodes (async)
    broadcast({:dialog_stored, dialog_id, state, node()})
    :ok
  end
  
  # Async store via GenServer cast
  @impl true  
  def store_async(dialog_id, state) do
    GenServer.cast(__MODULE__, {:store_async, dialog_id, state})
    :ok
  end
  
  def handle_cast({:store_async, dialog_id, state}, state_data) do
    store(dialog_id, state)
    {:noreply, state_data}
  end
  
  @impl true
  def fetch(dialog_id) do
    case :ets.lookup(@table, dialog_id) do
      [{^dialog_id, state, _node, _time}] -> {:ok, state}
      [] -> {:error, :not_found}
    end
  end
  
  @impl true
  def delete(dialog_id) do
    :ets.delete(@table, dialog_id)
    broadcast({:dialog_deleted, dialog_id, node()})
    :ok
  end
  
  @impl true
  def list_by_node(target_node) do
    :ets.select(@table, [{{:_, :\, target_node, :_}, [], [:\]}])
  end
  
  @impl true
  def list_all do
    :ets.select(@table, [{{:_, :\, :_, :_}, [], [:\]}])
  end
  
  @impl true
  def transfer_ownership(dialog_id, new_node) do
    case fetch(dialog_id) do
      {:ok, state} ->
        updated = %{state | owning_node: new_node, updated_at: DateTime.utc_now()}
        store(dialog_id, updated)
      error -> error
    end
  end
  
  # Handle incoming replication messages from other nodes
  def handle_info({:dialog_stored, dialog_id, state, from_node}, state_data) do
    # Don't process our own broadcasts
    if from_node \!= node() do
      record = {dialog_id, state, from_node, DateTime.utc_now()}
      :ets.insert(@table, record)
    end
    {:noreply, state_data}
  end
  
  def handle_info({:dialog_deleted, dialog_id, from_node}, state_data) do
    if from_node \!= node() do
      :ets.delete(@table, dialog_id)
    end
    {:noreply, state_data}
  end
  
  defp broadcast(message) do
    Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast(@pubsub, @topic, message)
  end
end
```

- Ignore self-broadcasts

title: Implement ClusterMonitor for node failure detection
description:
Create ClusterMonitor GenServer that detects node failures and triggers dialog recovery.

## Purpose
Detect when cluster nodes fail and trigger takeover of orphaned dialogs.

## Implementation

```elixir
defmodule ParrotSip.ClusterMonitor do
  @moduledoc """
  Monitors cluster nodes and triggers dialog recovery on node failure.
  
  When a node goes down:
  1. Query DialogStore for dialogs owned by dead node
  2. For each orphaned dialog, spawn a recovered DialogStatem
  3. Update ownership in DialogStore
  """
  
  use GenServer
  require Logger
  
  def start_link(opts) do
    GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, opts, name: __MODULE__)
  end
  
  def init(_opts) do
    # Monitor all nodes in the cluster
    :net_kernel.monitor_nodes(true, [:nodedown_reason])
    
    # Get current cluster state
    nodes = Node.list()
    Logger.info("ClusterMonitor started, connected to \#{length(nodes)} nodes")
    
    {:ok, %{nodes: nodes}}
  end
  
  def handle_info({:nodeup, node, _info}, state) do
    Logger.info("ClusterMonitor: Node joined: \#{node}")
    
    # Could trigger state sync here if needed
    
    {:noreply, %{state | nodes: [node | state.nodes]}}
  end
  
  def handle_info({:nodedown, dead_node, info}, state) do
    Logger.warning("ClusterMonitor: Node down: \#{dead_node}, reason: \#{inspect(info)}")
    
    # Recover dialogs owned by dead node
    recover_dialogs_from_node(dead_node)
    
    {:noreply, %{state | nodes: List.delete(state.nodes, dead_node)}}
  end
  
  defp recover_dialogs_from_node(dead_node) do
    orphaned_dialogs = ParrotSip.DialogStore.list_by_node(dead_node)
    
    Logger.info("ClusterMonitor: Recovering \#{length(orphaned_dialogs)} dialogs from \#{dead_node}")
    
    for dialog_state <- orphaned_dialogs do
      case ParrotSip.DialogStatem.start_recovered(dialog_state) do
        {:ok, pid} ->
          Logger.info("ClusterMonitor: Recovered dialog \#{dialog_state.id} as \#{inspect(pid)}")
          ParrotSip.DialogStore.transfer_ownership(dialog_state.id, node())
          
        {:error, reason} ->
          Logger.error("ClusterMonitor: Failed to recover dialog \#{dialog_state.id}: \#{inspect(reason)}")
      end
    end
  end
end
```

## Files to create
- apps/parrot_sip/lib/parrot_sip/cluster_monitor.ex

## Files to modify
- apps/parrot_sip/lib/parrot_sip/application.ex (add to supervision tree)

## Tests
- Test nodedown handling triggers recovery
- Test recovered dialogs are functional
- Test ownership transfer

## Notes
- Only one node should recover each dialog (use distributed lock or leader election for large clusters)
- For simple 2-3 node clusters, race conditions are acceptable (duplicate recovery is idempotent)

title: Add dialog recovery capability to DialogStatem
description:
Add the ability to start a DialogStatem from recovered state.

## Purpose
When a node fails, ClusterMonitor needs to spawn DialogStatem processes from stored state.

## Changes to DialogStatem

### Add start_recovered/1 function
```elixir
@doc """
Start a DialogStatem from recovered state (used during node failure recovery).
"""
@spec start_recovered(map()) :: GenStatem.on_start()
def start_recovered(stored_state) do
  ParrotSip.Dialog.Supervisor.start_child({:recover, stored_state})
end
```

### Add recovery init clause
```elixir
def init({:recover, stored_state}) do
  # Deserialize stored state back to Dialog struct
  dialog = deserialize_dialog(stored_state)
  
  # Re-register in local registry
  case Registry.register(ParrotSip.Registry, dialog.id, self()) do
    {:ok, _} -> :ok
    {:error, {:already_registered, _}} ->
      # Another process already recovered this dialog
      Logger.warning("Dialog \#{dialog.id} already recovered by another process")
      {:stop, :already_recovered}
  end
  
  # Rebuild any necessary timers based on state
  timers = rebuild_timers(dialog)
  
  Logger.info("[DialogStatem] Recovered dialog \#{dialog.id} in state \#{dialog.state}")
  
  # Notify owner if configured
  notify_owner_of_recovery(dialog)
  
  {:ok, dialog.state, %{
    dialog: dialog,
    timers: timers,
    recovered: true,
    recovered_at: DateTime.utc_now()
  }}
end

defp deserialize_dialog(stored_state) do
  %ParrotSip.Dialog{
    id: stored_state.id,
    state: stored_state.state,
    call_id: stored_state.call_id,
    local_tag: stored_state.local_tag,
    remote_tag: stored_state.remote_tag,
    local_uri: stored_state.local_uri,
    remote_uri: stored_state.remote_uri,
    remote_target: stored_state.remote_target,
    local_seq: stored_state.local_seq,
    remote_seq: stored_state.remote_seq,
    route_set: stored_state.route_set
  }
end

defp rebuild_timers(%{state: :confirmed} = dialog) do
  # In confirmed state, we might need session timers
  # RFC 4028 Session-Expires handling
  []
end

defp rebuild_timers(_dialog) do
  []
end

defp notify_owner_of_recovery(dialog) do
  # If there's an owner process registered, notify it
  case Registry.lookup(ParrotSip.Registry, {:dialog_owner, dialog.id}) do
    [{owner_pid, _}] ->
      send(owner_pid, {:dialog_recovered, dialog.id, self()})
    [] ->
      :ok
  end
end
```

- Test duplicate recovery is handled gracefully

title: Integrate DialogStore persistence into DialogStatem
description:
Modify DialogStatem to persist state changes to DialogStore.

## Purpose
Ensure dialog state is replicated to other nodes by writing to DialogStore on state changes.

## Changes to DialogStatem

### Persist on dialog creation (init)
```elixir
def init({:uas, request, response}) do
  {:ok, dialog} = Dialog.uas_create(request, response)
  
  # SYNC persist - must complete before we continue
  # This ensures state is replicated before 200 OK is sent
  :ok = DialogStore.store(dialog.id, serialize_dialog(dialog))
  
  # Register in local registry
  Registry.register(ParrotSip.Registry, dialog.id, self())
  
  {:ok, :confirmed, %{dialog: dialog}}
end
```

### Persist on state transitions (async)
```elixir
defp persist_state_change(dialog) do
  # ASYNC persist - don't block the state machine
  DialogStore.store_async(dialog.id, serialize_dialog(dialog))
end

# Example: handling in-dialog request
def confirmed(:cast, {:uas_request, msg}, data) do
  {:ok, updated_dialog} = Dialog.uas_process(msg, data.dialog)
  
  # Async persist
  persist_state_change(updated_dialog)
  
  {:keep_state, %{data | dialog: updated_dialog}}
end
```

### Delete on termination
```elixir
def terminate(_reason, _state, data) do
  DialogStore.delete(data.dialog.id)
  :ok
end
```

### Serialization helper
```elixir
defp serialize_dialog(%Dialog{} = dialog) do
  %{
    id: dialog.id,
    state: dialog.state,
    call_id: dialog.call_id,
    local_tag: dialog.local_tag,
    remote_tag: dialog.remote_tag,
    local_uri: dialog.local_uri,
    remote_uri: dialog.remote_uri,
    remote_target: dialog.remote_target,
    local_seq: dialog.local_seq,
    remote_seq: dialog.remote_seq,
    route_set: dialog.route_set,
    owning_node: node(),
    created_at: dialog.created_at || DateTime.utc_now(),
    updated_at: DateTime.utc_now()
  }
end
```

title: Implement Dialog Replication and BYE on Hangup
description:
Enable proper call termination by implementing dialog state replication (Phoenix.PubSub / DMQ style) and making hangup() send BYE requests.

## Architecture Decision
- Dialogs: ParrotSip manages with in-memory replication via Phoenix.PubSub
- Registrations: User application manages via RegistrationHandler callbacks (already implemented)

## Key Changes
1. Transaction.Server.response/2 returns {:ok, final_response} so caller knows the To tag
2. Add Phoenix.PubSub-based dialog replication for cluster support
3. Bridge.ActionExecutor captures dialog_id after answer
4. execute_hangup looks up dialog and sends BYE via Transaction.Client

## References
- Similar to Kamailio DMQ dialog replication
- Phoenix.Presence pattern for CRDT-style replication

title: Modify Transaction.Server.response/2 to return final response
description:
Change the API of Transaction.Server.response/2 to return {:ok, final_response} instead of :ok.

## Why
When sending a 2xx response to INVITE, the transaction layer adds a To tag (line 200 in transaction/server.ex). The caller needs to know this tag to compute the dialog_id.

## Current API
```elixir
:ok = Transaction.Server.response(response, uas)
```

## New API
```elixir
{:ok, final_response} = Transaction.Server.response(response, uas)
# final_response.to.parameters["tag"] contains the generated tag
```

## Files to modify
- apps/parrot_sip/lib/parrot_sip/transaction/server.ex - Change response/2 return value
- apps/parrot_sip/lib/parrot_sip/transaction_statem.ex - Propagate return value
- Update all callers in parrot_sip that call Transaction.Server.response/2

## Implementation notes
- The tag is already generated in make_reply/3 (lines 190-208)
- Need to return the modified response after tag is added
- This is a breaking API change but the right design

title: Integrate DialogStore into DialogStatem
description:
Modify DialogStatem to persist state changes to DialogStore.

## Changes to DialogStatem

### On dialog creation (init)
```elixir
def init({:uas, request, response}) do
  {:ok, dialog} = Dialog.uas_create(request, response)
  
  # Persist to store (async broadcast to other nodes)
  DialogStore.store(dialog.id, serialize(dialog))
  
  {:ok, :confirmed, %{dialog: dialog}}
end
```

### On state changes
```elixir
def confirmed(:cast, {:uas_request, msg}, data) do
  updated_dialog = process_request(data.dialog, msg)
  
  # Persist change (async)
  DialogStore.store_async(updated_dialog.id, serialize(updated_dialog))
  
  {:keep_state, %{data | dialog: updated_dialog}}
end
```

### On termination
```elixir
def terminate(_reason, _state, data) do
  DialogStore.delete(data.dialog.id)
  :ok
end
```

### Add recovery start function
```elixir
def start_recovered(stored_state) do
  GenStatem.start_link(__MODULE__, {:recover, stored_state})
end

def init({:recover, stored_state}) do
  dialog = deserialize(stored_state)
  Registry.register(ParrotSip.Registry, dialog.id, self())
  {:ok, dialog.state, %{dialog: dialog}}
end
```

## Files to modify
- apps/parrot_sip/lib/parrot_sip/dialog_statem.ex

## Async writes
Use cast to a writer process for non-critical updates to avoid blocking the state machine. Dialog creation should be sync to ensure stored before 200 OK sent.

title: Capture dialog_id in ActionExecutor after answer
description:
Modify execute_answer to capture the dialog_id from the final response and store it in the Call struct.

## Context
After Task ezy.1, Transaction.Server.response/2 returns {:ok, final_response} with the To tag added. We need to use this to compute the dialog_id.

## Changes to ActionExecutor.execute_answer

```elixir
def execute_answer(call, %{sip_msg: sip_msg} = context, _opts) do
  Logger.debug("[ActionExecutor] Executing answer operation")
  
  # Build 200 OK response
  response = Message.reply(sip_msg, 200, "OK")
  
  # Send the response - now returns final response with To tag
  {:ok, final_response} = send_response(context, response)
  
  # Compute dialog_id from final response
  # For UAS: local_tag = To tag (us), remote_tag = From tag (them)
  dialog_id = compute_dialog_id(sip_msg, final_response)
  
  # Update call state with dialog_id
  updated_call = call
    |> Map.put(:state, :answered)
    |> Map.put(:__dialog_id__, dialog_id)
  
  {:ok, updated_call}
end

defp compute_dialog_id(request, response) do
  call_id = request.call_id
  
  # From tag (remote party) from request
  remote_tag = get_in(request, [:from, :parameters, "tag"])
  
  # To tag (us) from response
  local_tag = get_in(response, [:to, :parameters, "tag"])
  
  # Generate dialog ID from UAS perspective
  ParrotSip.Dialog.generate_id(:uas, call_id, local_tag, remote_tag)
end
```

## Files to modify
- apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/action_executor.ex

## Dependencies
- Requires ezy.1 (Transaction.Server returns final response)

## Tests
- Test dialog_id is set in Call after answer
- Test dialog_id matches expected format

title: Implement execute_hangup to send BYE via dialog
description:
Modify execute_hangup to look up the dialog and send a BYE request.

## Current Behavior
```elixir
def execute_hangup(call, %{media_pid: media_pid} = _context) do
  # Only stops media - does NOT send BYE
  if media_pid, do: send(media_pid, {:stop_media})
  updated_call = %{call | state: :terminated}
  {:ok, updated_call}
end
```

## New Implementation
```elixir
def execute_hangup(call, context) do
  Logger.debug("[ActionExecutor] Executing hangup operation")
  
  # 1. Stop media session if running
  if context.media_pid do
    send(context.media_pid, {:stop_media})
  end
  
  # 2. Send BYE if we have a dialog
  case call.__dialog_id__ do
    nil ->
      Logger.warning("[ActionExecutor] No dialog_id, cannot send BYE")
      
    dialog_id ->
      send_bye(dialog_id, call)
  end
  
  # 3. Update call state
  updated_call = %{call | state: :terminated}
  {:ok, updated_call}
end

defp send_bye(dialog_id, call) do
  # Look up dialog from store
  case ParrotSip.DialogStore.fetch(dialog_id) do
    {:ok, dialog_state} ->
      # Create BYE message using dialog
      {:ok, bye_msg, _updated_dialog} = ParrotSip.Dialog.uac_request(:bye, dialog_state)
      
      # Send BYE via client transaction
      test_pid = self()
      callback = fn result ->
        send(test_pid, {:bye_result, call.id, result})
      end
      
      {:uac_id, _trans} = ParrotSip.Transaction.Client.request(bye_msg, callback)
      
      Logger.info("[ActionExecutor] Sent BYE for call \#{call.id}")
      :ok
      
    {:error, :not_found} ->
      Logger.warning("[ActionExecutor] Dialog \#{dialog_id} not found, cannot send BYE")
      :ok
  end
end
```

## Alternative: Use DialogStatem directly
If the dialog process is still running, we could send a message to it:
```elixir
defp send_bye(dialog_id, _call) do
  case ParrotSip.DialogStatem.send_bye(dialog_id) do
    :ok -> :ok
    {:error, reason} -> Logger.warning("Failed to send BYE: \#{inspect(reason)}")
  end
end
```

This would require adding send_bye/1 to DialogStatem.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<scenario name="UAC expects server-initiated BYE">
  <!-- Send INVITE -->
  <send>
    <![CDATA[
      INVITE sip:test@[remote_ip]:[remote_port] SIP/2.0
      Via: SIP/2.0/UDP [local_ip]:[local_port];branch=[branch]
      From: <sip:sipp@[local_ip]:[local_port]>;tag=[call_number]
      To: <sip:test@[remote_ip]:[remote_port]>
      Call-ID: [call_id]
      CSeq: 1 INVITE
      Contact: <sip:sipp@[local_ip]:[local_port]>
      Content-Length: 0
    ]]>
  </send>
  
  <!-- Expect 200 OK -->
  <recv response="200"/>
  
  <!-- Send ACK -->
  <send>
    <![CDATA[
      ACK sip:test@[remote_ip]:[remote_port] SIP/2.0
      Via: SIP/2.0/UDP [local_ip]:[local_port];branch=[branch]
      From: <sip:sipp@[local_ip]:[local_port]>;tag=[call_number]
      To: <sip:test@[remote_ip]:[remote_port]>[peer_tag_param]
      Call-ID: [call_id]
      CSeq: 1 ACK
      Content-Length: 0
    ]]>
  </send>
  
  <!-- Expect BYE from server -->
  <recv request="BYE"/>
  
  <!-- Send 200 OK to BYE -->
  <send>
    <![CDATA[
      SIP/2.0 200 OK
      [last_Via:]
      [last_From:]
      [last_To:]
      [last_Call-ID:]
      [last_CSeq:]
      Content-Length: 0
    ]]>
  </send>
</scenario>
```

title: B2BUA Call Bridging
description:
Implement B2BUA (Back-to-Back User Agent) for call bridging and forking.

**Required Operations:**
- :bridge - Bridge call to single destination
- :fork - Fork call to multiple destinations

**Architecture:**

1. UAC Capabilities:
   - Create outgoing INVITE from bridge handler
   - Handle early media (183 Session Progress)
   - Handle final responses
   - Manage B-leg dialog lifecycle

2. Media Bridging:
   - Connect A-leg and B-leg media streams
   - Handle codec transcoding if needed
   - Support hold/unhold

3. Signaling Bridging:
   - Relay BYE in both directions
   - Handle re-INVITEs
   - Support REFER for transfer

**Dependencies:**
- cz5: Dialog State Replication (for B-leg dialog management)
- ezy: BYE on Hangup (for proper termination)

**Key Files:**
- apps/parrot_sip/lib/parrot_sip/ua.ex (UAC)
- apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/b2bua.ex (new)
- apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/action_executor.ex

**RFC References:**
- RFC 3261 Section 16 (Proxy Behavior has B2BUA patterns)
- RFC 5765 (B2BUA Requirements)

title: Create Parrot.Bridge.B2BUA module
description:
Create the B2BUA coordinator module.

Responsibilities:
- Coordinate A-leg and B-leg calls
- Handle state transitions
- Manage media bridging
- Route signaling between legs

State Machine States:
- :setup - Creating B-leg
- :early_media - B-leg ringing (183)
- :connected - Both legs answered
- :terminating - One leg hanging up
- :terminated - Both legs down

Key Functions:
- start_link(a_leg_call, destination, opts)
- handle_b_leg_response(b2bua, response)
- handle_bye(b2bua, leg)
- bridge_media(b2bua)

File: apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/b2bua.ex

title: Implement :bridge operation in ActionExecutor
description:
Implement execute_bridge/4 in ActionExecutor.

Operation: {:bridge, destination, opts}

Options:
- :timeout - Timeout in milliseconds
- :headers - Custom headers for B-leg INVITE
- :handler - B-leg handler module

Implementation:
1. Add execute_bridge/4 function
2. Verify call is in :answered state
3. Start B2BUA.start_link(call, destination, opts)
4. Return {:ok, updated_call, :stop}

Note: Bridge is a terminal operation - execution stops here.
B2BUA manages both legs from this point.

Dependencies: q90.1 (B2BUA module)

File: apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/action_executor.ex

title: Implement :fork operation in ActionExecutor
description:
Implement execute_fork/4 in ActionExecutor.

Operation: {:fork, destinations, opts}

Options:
- :strategy - :first_answer or :ring_all
- :timeout - Timeout in milliseconds

Implementation:
1. Add execute_fork/4 function
2. Verify call is in :answered state
3. Start multiple B-leg INVITEs (via B2BUA or ForkController)
4. Handle strategy:
   - :first_answer - Cancel others when one answers
   - :ring_all - Wait for all to ring before connecting
5. Return {:ok, updated_call, :stop}

Dependencies: q90.1 (B2BUA module)

title: Parrot DSL Framework
description: Phoenix-style DSL for building VoIP applications. New parrot app that sits on top of parrot_sip and parrot_media. See docs/plans/2026-01-06-parrot-dsl-design.md

title: Create parrot app scaffold
description: Create the new parrot app in the umbrella with proper deps on parrot_sip and parrot_media

title: Create parrot app with mix new
description: Run mix new apps/parrot --sup to create the new app in the umbrella

title: Add deps on parrot_sip and parrot_media
description: Update apps/parrot/mix.exs to depend on parrot_sip and parrot_media

title: Create Parrot module with child_spec
description: Create lib/parrot.ex with child_spec for application startup: {Parrot, router: MyRouter, transports: [...]}

title: Call struct and pipeline builder
description: Implement Parrot.Call struct with assigns and pipeline builder functions (answer, play, bridge, etc.)

title: Define Parrot.Call struct with assigns, from, to, method fields
description: Create the core Call struct used throughout the DSL. Must support: assigns map for per-call state, from/to URIs, method, and internal refs. See MiniPBX handlers using call.assigns pattern.

title: Implement signaling operations: answer/0, answer/1, reject/1, hangup/0
description: Pipeline ops for call signaling. answer() sends 200 OK, answer(sdp_opts) with custom SDP, reject(status) sends error response, hangup() sends BYE. Used in MiniPBX: invite |> answer() |> play()

title: Implement assign/2 for per-call state
description: Like Phoenix conn.assigns. call |> assign(:menu, :main) |> assign(:retries, 0). Used throughout MiniPBX for IVR state, caller info, recording filenames.

title: Implement playback operations: play/1, play/2
description: play(file) plays single file, play([files]) plays sequence, play(file, loop: true) loops. Triggers handle_play_complete callback when done. Used in MiniPBX auto-attendant menu.

title: Implement recording operations: record/2, stop_record/0
description: record(path, opts) with max_duration, terminators, beep options. Triggers handle_record_complete(path, duration, call). Used in MiniPBX voicemail.

title: Implement DTMF collection: collect_dtmf/1
description: collect_dtmf(max: 4, timeout: 5000, terminators: ["#"]). Triggers handle_dtmf(digits, call) or handle_dtmf(:timeout, call). Used in MiniPBX IVR menus.

title: Implement prompt/2 combining play with collect_dtmf
description: prompt(file, collect: [max: 4, timeout: 10000]) plays then collects. Triggers handle_prompt_complete(file, digits, call). Convenience combo.

title: Implement bridging: bridge/2
description: bridge(dest, timeout: 30000, headers: %{}, handler: BLegHandler). Creates B-leg, triggers handle_bridge_complete(:answered|{:failed, reason}, call). Used in MiniPBX extension calls.

title: Implement forking: fork/2
description: fork(destinations, strategy: :first_answer, timeout: 30000). Ring multiple endpoints, first answer wins. Triggers handle_fork_complete({:answered, winner}|:no_answer, call). Used in MiniPBX ring-all.

title: Router DSL
description: Phoenix-style router with scope, pipeline, plug macros and IP/header/from/to matching

title: Create use Parrot.Router macro with __using__ callback
description: Macro that sets up router module with necessary imports, module attributes for storing routes, and compiles route table at compile time. See MiniPBX.Router example.

title: Implement pipeline/2 macro for defining named pipelines
description: pipeline :authenticated do plug :verify_registration end. Stores plugs in module attribute, executed in order when pipe_through called.

title: Implement scope/2 macro with matching options
description: scope opts do ... end. Match options: from_ip (CIDR/list), from (URI pattern), to (pattern), header ({name, value}). Nested routes inherit scope conditions.

title: Implement pipe_through/1 macro
description: pipe_through :authenticated - applies named pipeline to all routes in current scope. Multiple pipe_through calls accumulate.

title: Implement invite/2 macro for INVITE routing
description: invite pattern, HandlerModule - routes matching INVITEs to handler. Patterns: "1xx" (extension range), "9~" (prefix match), "*" (catch-all). Used in MiniPBX for extension/outbound/inbound routing.

title: Implement register/1 and presence/1 macros for handler assignment
description: register MyRegistrationHandler - assigns REGISTER handler. presence MyPresenceHandler - assigns SUBSCRIBE/PUBLISH handler. Global assignments, not scoped.

title: Implement IP matching with CIDR support
description: from_ip: "192.168.0.0/16" - match source IP against CIDR. Support list: from_ip: ["10.0.0.1", "10.0.0.2"]. Used to distinguish internal vs trunk traffic.

title: Implement route dispatch function
description: Generate dispatch/1 function at compile time that pattern matches incoming requests against route table, runs plugs, returns handler module.

title: InviteHandler behaviour
description: Behaviour for call handlers with defined callbacks: handle_invite, handle_play_complete, handle_dtmf, handle_bridge_complete, etc.

title: Define Parrot.InviteHandler behaviour with @callback specs
description: Define behaviour module with @callback for all InviteHandler functions. Provides typespec documentation for implementers.

title: Implement sensible default callbacks
description: Default implementations: bridge_failed -> play tone, hangup. no_answer -> play tone, hangup. Implementers override only what they need.

title: Implement use Parrot.InviteHandler macro with defaults
description: Macro that injects @behaviour, imports pipeline ops, provides default implementations. Implementers override only what they need.

title: Implement handle_invite/1 callback routing
description: Entry point when INVITE routed to handler. def handle_invite(invite) - returns pipeline result. See MiniPBX.Handlers.ExtensionsHandler.

title: Implement handle_play_complete/2 callback
description: Called when playback finishes. def handle_play_complete(filename, call) - pattern match on filename to determine next action. Used extensively in MiniPBX IVRs.

title: Implement handle_dtmf/2 callback
description: Called when DTMF collected. def handle_dtmf(digits, call) or def handle_dtmf(:timeout, call). Pattern match on digits and call.assigns for menu state.

title: Implement handle_bridge_complete/2 callback
description: Called when bridge ends. def handle_bridge_complete(:answered, call) or {:failed, reason}. Reasons: :busy, :no_answer, :rejected. Used for voicemail fallback.

title: Implement handle_fork_complete/2 callback
description: Called when fork ends. def handle_fork_complete({:answered, winner}, call) or :no_answer. Winner contains URI of endpoint that answered first.

title: Implement handle_record_complete/3 callback
description: Called when recording finishes. def handle_record_complete(filepath, duration, call). Used in MiniPBX voicemail to save message metadata.

title: Implement handle_hangup/1 callback
description: Called when call ends. def handle_hangup(call). Cleanup, presence updates. Returns {:noreply, call}. Used in MiniPBX to update presence on call end.

title: RegistrationHandler behaviour
description: Behaviour for registration handlers: authenticate, store_binding, get_bindings. Framework handles digest auth.

title: Define Parrot.RegistrationHandler behaviour
description: Define behaviour with @callback specs for authenticate/1, store_binding/3, get_bindings/1, handle_registration_expired/1.

title: Implement use Parrot.RegistrationHandler macro
description: Macro that injects @behaviour and imports helpers. Framework handles SIP mechanics (401 challenge/response), user provides business logic.

title: Implement authenticate/1 callback
description: def authenticate(credentials) returns :ok | :error. Credentials struct has username, password, realm. See MiniPBX.Handlers.RegistrationHandler.

title: Implement store_binding/3 callback
description: def store_binding(aor, contact, expires) - called after successful auth. Store in user storage (Mnesia/ETS/DB). Trigger presence update.

title: Implement get_bindings/1 callback
description: def get_bindings(aor) - returns list of current contacts for 200 OK response. Filter expired bindings.

title: Implement handle_registration_expired/1 callback
description: def handle_registration_expired(aor) - called when registration expires. Update presence to offline, cleanup.

title: Implement 401 challenge/response flow in framework
description: Framework handles: receive REGISTER, send 401 with nonce, receive REGISTER with auth, verify digest, call authenticate callback. User never sees SIP mechanics.

title: Implement registration expiry timer management
description: Framework tracks registration expiry, calls handle_registration_expired when binding expires. Use erlang timer or gen_statem timeout.

title: PresenceHandler behaviour
description: Behaviour for presence: authorize_subscription, store_subscription, get_presence, handle_publish. Plus Parrot.Presence.notify/2 API.

title: Define Parrot.PresenceHandler behaviour
description: Define behaviour with @callback specs for authorize_subscription/2, store_subscription/1, get_subscriptions/1, get_presence/1, handle_publish/2.

title: Implement use Parrot.PresenceHandler macro
description: Macro that injects @behaviour. Framework handles SUBSCRIBE/NOTIFY/PUBLISH SIP mechanics, user provides authorization and storage.

title: Implement authorize_subscription/2 callback
description: def authorize_subscription(watcher, presentity) returns :allow | :deny | :pending. Pending for approval flows. See MiniPBX allowing internal users to watch each other.

title: Implement store_subscription/1 and get_subscriptions/1 callbacks
description: Store/retrieve subscriptions from user storage. get_subscriptions(presentity) returns list of watchers to notify on state change.

title: Implement get_presence/1 callback
description: def get_presence(presentity) returns %{status: :open|:closed, note: string}. Maps internal state (:available/:busy/:offline) to presence document.

title: Implement handle_publish/2 callback
description: def handle_publish(presentity, presence_state) - called when user publishes their state. Store in user storage.

title: Implement Parrot.Presence.notify/2 API
description: Async fire-and-forget function: Parrot.Presence.notify(presentity, %{status: :busy}). Looks up watchers, sends NOTIFY to each. Called from call handlers.

title: Implement SUBSCRIBE/NOTIFY/PUBLISH SIP flow in framework
description: Framework handles: receive SUBSCRIBE, call authorize, store subscription, send 200/202. On notify/2, lookup watchers, send NOTIFY with presence XML.

title: BLegHandler behaviour
description: Advanced B-leg control: before_invite, handle_provisional, handle_answer, handle_reject callbacks

title: Define Parrot.BLegHandler behaviour
description: Define behaviour for power users needing full B-leg control. Callbacks for INVITE manipulation, provisional handling, answer/reject, in-dialog events.

title: Implement use Parrot.BLegHandler macro
description: Macro with sensible defaults. Most users never need this - only for advanced B2BUA scenarios requiring header manipulation or early media control.

title: Implement before_invite/2 callback for INVITE manipulation
description: def before_invite(invite, state) - modify INVITE before sending. put_header, remove_header, modify_sdp. Returns modified invite.

title: Implement handle_provisional/2 callback
description: Handle 180/183 responses. Return {:ring, bleg} for ring-back, {:early_media, bleg} to connect early media to A-leg.

title: Implement handle_answer/2 and handle_reject/2 callbacks
description: handle_answer returns {:connect, bleg} to bridge media. handle_reject returns {:rejected, status, bleg}. Framework then notifies A-leg handler.

title: Implement handle_reinvite/2 and handle_bye/2 callbacks
description: In-dialog event handling. reinvite returns {:passthrough, bleg} to forward to A-leg. bye returns {:hangup, bleg}.

title: Implement header manipulation helpers
description: put_header/3, remove_header/2, modify_sdp/2 helpers for before_invite. Clean API for common B2BUA operations.
